extra.reasoning: The mutual-exclusion property requires that rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b never be jointly true. rec_drug_b is set true only by r1 (IF hepatic_impairment). rec_drug_a is set true only by r4 (IF hepatic_impairment AND ckd_dialysis AND immunosuppressed AND heart_failure AND pregnancy). r4's antecedent includes hepatic_impairment, so every input that fires r4 also necessarily fires r1. No other rule writes rec_drug_a or rec_drug_b, and priorities/defaults cannot unset a value a matching rule has set to true (defaults only apply to outputs left unset). Therefore the input with all five of r4's conjuncts true (hepatic_impairment, ckd_dialysis, immunosuppressed, heart_failure, pregnancy = true; qt_prolongation, elderly arbitrary, here false) fires both r1 and r4, yielding rec_drug_a = true and rec_drug_b = true simultaneously, violating the property.
